requirement.statement: The system SHALL provide a platform-neutral sandbox backend runtime for command execution across macOS, Linux, and Windows.

case.name: Platform-specific backend selection
case.given: a Bash tool execution with runtime metadata containing `sandbox_permissions`
case.when: the runtime resolves backend selection
case.then: macOS SHALL select the Seatbelt backend for `read-only` or `workspace-write`
Linux SHALL select the Linux sandbox backend for `read-only` or `workspace-write`
Windows SHALL select the Windows sandbox backend for `read-only` or `workspace-write`
`danger-full-access` SHALL select the unsandboxed backend on every platform.

case.name: Bash execution uses backend selection
case.given: a Bash tool execution with runtime metadata containing sandbox permissions
case.when: the command is executed
case.then: the tool SHALL delegate process execution to the sandbox backend runtime
SHALL preserve existing local permission authorization before executing the command.

case.name: Existing permission chain is preserved
case.given: a Bash tool execution requires local permission authorization
case.when: the command is executed through any sandbox backend
case.then: the tool SHALL call local permission authorization before resolving or invoking the sandbox backend
SHALL keep existing dangerous command guards.

case.name: Writable roots are resolved consistently
case.given: `workspace-write` mode and runtime metadata with additional writable roots or workspace-access grants
case.when: backend selection is resolved
case.then: the selected platform backend SHALL receive a normalized unique list of writable roots
read-only mode SHALL receive no writable roots.

case.name: Workspace access grants extend sandbox writable roots
case.given: `workspace-access.json` grants write access to a directory outside the workspace for the current workspace
the platform is macOS with `sandbox_mode` set to `workspace-write`
case.when: a Bash command is executed through the sandbox backend
case.then: the macOS Seatbelt writable roots SHALL include the granted directory
SHALL continue to protect workspace metadata directories from generic writes.

case.name: Dangerous mode remains unsandboxed
case.given: runtime metadata has `sandbox_mode` set to `danger-full-access`
case.when: a Bash command is executed
case.then: the runtime SHALL execute without wrapping the command in a sandbox backend
SHALL retain existing dangerous command guards.

requirement.statement: The system SHALL provide a macOS Seatbelt backend that wraps shell commands with `/usr/bin/sandbox-exec`.

case.name: workspace-write restricts writes to allowed roots
case.given: the platform is macOS and `sandbox_mode` is `workspace-write`
the workspace root and additional writable roots are known
case.when: a Bash command is executed
case.then: the command SHALL run under a Seatbelt policy that permits writes only under those writable roots
SHALL protect workspace metadata directories from generic writes.

case.name: read-only blocks writes
case.given: the platform is macOS and `sandbox_mode` is `read-only`
case.when: a Bash command attempts filesystem writes
case.then: the Seatbelt policy SHALL deny those writes.

case.name: network follows runtime metadata
case.given: the platform is macOS and `network_access` is disabled
case.when: a Bash command is executed through the sandbox backend
case.then: the generated Seatbelt policy SHALL omit broad outbound and inbound network permissions.

requirement.statement: The system SHALL provide a Linux backend modeled on Codex's bubblewrap plus seccomp sandbox design.

case.name: Linux read-only blocks writes
case.given: the platform is Linux and `sandbox_mode` is `read-only`
case.when: Bash execution attempts filesystem writes
case.then: the Linux backend SHALL run the command in a read-only filesystem view
the write attempt SHALL fail without modifying the target path.

case.name: Linux workspace-write permits only writable roots
case.given: the platform is Linux and `sandbox_mode` is `workspace-write`
workspace and additional writable roots are known
case.when: Bash execution writes inside an allowed writable root
case.then: the write SHALL succeed
case.when: Bash execution writes outside the writable roots
case.then: the write SHALL fail.

case.name: Linux protected metadata remains protected
case.given: a writable root contains protected metadata directories
case.when: a sandboxed Linux command attempts to write protected metadata paths
case.then: the backend SHALL deny or mask those writes.

case.name: Linux network policy is enforced or explicitly rejected
case.given: the platform is Linux and `network_access` is `disabled`
case.when: Bash execution attempts network access
case.then: the backend SHALL isolate or block network access
if the required namespace or helper capability is unavailable, execution SHALL fail with a clear sandbox setup error.

requirement.statement: The system SHALL provide a Windows backend modeled on Codex's elevated restricted-token and ACL sandbox design.

case.name: Windows read-only uses restricted identity
case.given: the platform is Windows and `sandbox_mode` is `read-only`
case.when: Bash execution runs a command
case.then: the Windows backend SHALL run it under a restricted sandbox identity or token
filesystem writes outside required runtime scratch locations SHALL fail.

case.name: Windows workspace-write uses capability-scoped writable roots
case.given: the platform is Windows and `sandbox_mode` is `workspace-write`
writable roots are known
case.when: Bash execution writes inside an allowed writable root
case.then: the write SHALL succeed
case.when: Bash execution writes outside allowed writable roots
case.then: the write SHALL fail.

case.name: Windows setup state is explicit
case.given: Windows sandbox setup is required before command execution
case.when: the setup has not completed or required privileges are unavailable
case.then: the runtime SHALL return an actionable setup error
SHALL NOT silently downgrade to unsandboxed execution.

case.name: Windows network policy is enforced or explicitly rejected
case.given: the platform is Windows and `network_access` is `disabled`
case.when: Bash execution requests a sandboxed command
case.then: the backend SHALL apply the configured Windows network restriction
if network filtering is unavailable, execution SHALL fail with a clear degraded-state message rather than allowing unrestricted network access.

requirement.statement: The system SHALL preserve explicit fallback behavior without silently weakening requested sandbox permissions.

case.name: Unsupported backend reports a degraded or unsupported state
case.given: the host platform requests `read-only` or `workspace-write`
the required platform backend dependency is unavailable
case.when: Bash execution attempts to run
case.then: the runtime SHALL return a clear error or degraded-state message
SHALL NOT silently run the command unsandboxed.

case.name: macOS behavior remains unchanged
case.given: the platform is macOS
case.when: a command is executed under `read-only`, `workspace-write`, or `danger-full-access`
case.then: existing Seatbelt and unsandboxed behavior SHALL continue to pass current sandbox backend tests.
